prompt:
Use case: stylized-concept
Asset type: project-bound RPG 1920s cosmic-horror atlas map, first batch pass, landscape orientation
Primary request: Call of Cthulhu / 1920s Lovecraftian inspired world map, single huge primary continent.
Scene/backdrop: overhead birds-eye aged parchment atlas view of a fog-shrouded 1920s coastline and hinterland, surrounded by dark cold ocean.
Subject: one enormous primary continent with complex realistic coastline: New England-style rocky peninsulas, misty gulfs, bays, capes, narrow straits, broken shores, curved coasts, marshy river deltas, inland lakes, and varied landforms. Include Arkham-like university towns, Miskatonic river delta, old colonial architecture, lighthouse coasts, foggy hills, ancient standing stones, forbidden cult sites, graveyards, drowned coves, forests, wetlands, tundra, mountains, and bleak coastal regions.
Style/medium: 1920s New England town map expanded to continent scale, Lovecraftian horror, aged parchment with hand annotations but no readable text, occult cartography, highly detailed illustrated RPG sourcebook map.
Composition/framing: wide 16:9 landscape, full single continent visible with generous dark ocean border, large high-resolution atlas feel, top-down map clarity.
Lighting/mood: creeping cosmic dread, Jazz Age antiquarian mystery, fog, impossible shadows, ancient gods slumbering beneath earth and sea, sanity-eroding atmosphere.
Color palette: aged sepia parchment, cold grey-blue ocean, faded ink, sickly green occult accents, fog grey, dark pine forests, autumn browns.
Materials/textures: brittle paper, ink wash, etched coastlines, faint coffee stains, marginal occult sigils without readable lettering.
Constraints: no text labels, no letters, no readable words, no legend, no compass text, no watermark, no UI, no characters or portraits. Make the coastline natural, intricate, and realistic rather than a smooth blob.
